# Miscompilation of union containing __fp16 and _BitInt for big-endian targets

**Author:** ostannard
**URL:** https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/62260
**Status:** Closed
**Labels:** c23, miscompilation, generated by fuzzer
**Closed Date:** 2025-03-21T14:20:12Z

## Body

Clang generates incorrect code for function `foo` in this example, at all optimisation levels, and when targeting any big-endian architecture:

```c
union U1 {
  __fp16 M0;
  _BitInt(28) M1;
};

union U2 {
  __fp16 M0;
  int M1;
};

union U1 foo() {
  union U1 result = {1.0};
  return result;
}

union U2 bar() {
  union U2 result = {1.0};
  return result;
}
```

The encoding of 1.0 in FP16 is 0x3c00 (checked with https://evanw.github.io/float-toy/), so that must appear in the return value somewhere, but for `foo`, that value gets shifted and truncated to 0xc00:

```console
$ /work/llvm/build/bin/clang --target=arm-none-eabi -march=armv7-a -c test.c -o - -S -O1 -mbig-endian
...
foo:
        .fnstart
        mov     r0, #-1073741824
        bx      lr
...
bar:
        .fnstart
        mov     r0, #1006632960
        bx      lr
...
```

The generated code for AArch64 is similar:
```console
$ /work/llvm/build/bin/clang --target=aarch64-none-eabi -c test.c -o - -S -O1 -mbig-endian
...
foo:                                    // @foo
// %bb.0:                               // %entry
        mov     x0, #-4611686018427387904       // =0xc000000000000000
        ret
...
bar:                                    // @bar
// %bb.0:                               // %entry
        mov     x0, #4323455642275676160        // =0x3c00000000000000
        ret
...
```

PowerPC (big-endian) also has the same wrong constants in the output:
```console
$ /work/llvm/build/bin/clang --target=powerpc -c test.c -o - -S -O1
...
foo:                                    # @foo
.Lfunc_begin0:
        .cfi_startproc
# %bb.0:                                # %entry
        lis 3, -16384
...
        .type   bar,@function
bar:                                    # @bar
.Lfunc_begin1:
        .cfi_startproc
# %bb.0:                                # %entry
        lis 3, 15360
        blr
...
```

## Comments

### Comment 1 - llvmbot

@llvm/issue-subscribers-c2x

---

### Comment 2 - Fznamznon

The incorrect value appears after optimizations are applied, at -O0 `0x3c00` appears in both functions - https://godbolt.org/z/oYWaG83dq, for any mentioned big-endian architecture. The problem starts appearing in the IR after O1, see how the wrong value is returned by `foo()` - https://godbolt.org/z/b9KEMYcY4 . 
I don't think this is the problem in clang's lowering.
cc @AaronBallman .

---

### Comment 3 - AaronBallman

> I don't think this is the problem in clang's lowering.

The IR generated at -O0 looks sensible to me, so I think you're correct, this seems to be happening on the backend.

---

### Comment 4 - ostannard

This is no longer failing on `main`. I've re-run the fuzzer which first found it, and haven't hit any more failures.

```
$ /work/llvm/build/bin/clang --target=arm-none-eabi -march=armv7-a -c test.c -o - -S -O1 -mbig-endian
...
foo:
        .fnstart
@ %bb.0:                                @ %entry
        mov     r0, #1006632960
        bx      lr
.Lfunc_end0:
        .size   foo, .Lfunc_end0-foo
        .cantunwind
        .fnend
                                        @ -- End function
        .globl  bar                             @ -- Begin function bar
        .p2align        2
        .type   bar,%function
        .code   32                              @ @bar
bar:
        .fnstart
@ %bb.0:                                @ %entry
        mov     r0, #1006632960
        bx      lr
.Lfunc_end1:
...
```

```
$ /work/llvm/build/bin/clang --target=aarch64-none-elf -S test.c -o - -O1 -mbig-endian
...
foo:                                    // @foo
// %bb.0:                               // %entry
        mov     x0, #4323455642275676160        // =0x3c00000000000000
        ret
.Lfunc_end0:
        .size   foo, .Lfunc_end0-foo
                                        // -- End function
        .globl  bar                             // -- Begin function bar
        .p2align        2
        .type   bar,@function
bar:                                    // @bar
// %bb.0:                               // %entry
        mov     x0, #4323455642275676160        // =0x3c00000000000000
        ret
.Lfunc_end1:
...
```
